steps.title: Simplify both equations to standard form
steps.content:
Starting with the first equation:
$$4x - 9y = 9y + 5$$ Combine the $y$ terms on the left:
$$4x - 18y = 5$$
For the second equation:
$$hy = 2 + 4x$$ Rearrange to standard form:
$$-4x + hy = 2$$

steps.title: For no solution, lines must be parallel
steps.content:
Two linear equations have no solution when they represent parallel lines—same slope, different $y$-intercepts. This happens when the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ are proportional, but the constants are not.

Set up the proportion of coefficients:
$$\frac{4}{-4} = \frac{-18}{h}$$
Simplify the left side:
$$-1 = \frac{-18}{h}$$
Multiply both sides by $h$:
$$-h = -18$$ $$h = 18$$
Verify the constants are different: the first equation has constant \$5$, the second has constant \$2$. Since $5 \neq 2$, the lines are indeed parallel and distinct, confirming no solution. That matches choice D — D is correct.
